weather_report: Late morning in West Leederville rests under a solid, pewter lid of cloud, the light milk-washed and gentle, shadows erased. Expect the gray to linger into the afternoon, keeping the day cool and even-tempered rather than dour. Sunshine will be largely withheld; UV stays on the low side for winter thanks to the blanket above, so only a modest dab of sunscreen is needed if you’re out around midday. The air is clear but the world looks softened at the edges, as if seen through fine muslin—good visibility without the sparkle. It’s quietly agreeable weather for unhurried errands and small pleasures: a loop by Lake Monger to stretch the legs, a late coffee with a notebook, a tidy of the week’s paperwork, or a train into the city for an hour of browsing. Dress in light layers—a long-sleeve and a soft jacket you can unbutton—comfortable shoes, and perhaps a thin windbreaker if you’re cycling. Leave the umbrella; the clouds feel contemplative rather than ominous.
